clinical_history: The following ECG is most consistent with which clinical scenario?  50-year-old woman with....
diagnosis: c)  ECG shows classic signs of right atrial/right ventricular overload:  1) peaked P waves; 2) QR in V1 with a narrow QRS (not a true right bundle branch block); 3) delayed precordial transition zone, with terminal S waves in V5/V6; 4) superior axis deviation with an S1S2S3 pattern.  (Note that the P wave, while  predominantly negative in V1, is not broad, so this finding is not evidence of left or biatrial abnormality in this context.)
